title: ptelea trifoliata var. pumila
intro: Ptelea trifoliata var. pumila, also known as the dwarf hop tree, is a small shrub native to North America that belongs to the Rutaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which are attributed to its essential oils and alkaloids. The dwarf hop tree offers various health benefits, such as relief from digestive issues, reduction of fever, and treatment of respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Ptelea trifoliata var. pumila include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
